title: The Snake That Almost Never Wins This Fight
script: A king cobra can kill an elephant with one bite, but it loses almost every fight with this. The mongoose. It weighs four pounds, stands knee-high, and walks straight at snakes eighteen feet long. Its trick is not speed, it is biology: special receptors in its blood block the cobra's neurotoxin before it ever reaches the brain. So it lets the cobra strike first, dodges by inches, and wears the snake down in circles until it clamps onto the skull and does not let go. Farmers in India have watched this fight for generations, and the cobra almost never walks away. How does something this small keep beating something built to kill a bull elephant?
